I’m only 20 minutes by car (or Uber, on chemo days) from Derriford but Treliske is a full 90-minute drive. The biggest issue was working out how to do the bowel prep and get there for 7am for surgery. A friend picked me up the day before and gave me their spare bedroom/bathroom and lots of loo roll.

I liked the surgeon at Treliske (Truro) but not the ward or staffing conditions. Whereas Derriford seems very good with the whole oncology/chemo stuff. A friend who’s having chemo at Treliske was told they don’t offer the option of a cold cap because it takes too much time.

Almost the first thing she said was “this probably won’t cure you but it’s about giving you as long as possible before it comes back.” I guess it’s important to make it clear because it was such a big surgery that I’m sure at that point there’ll be women who decide just to do chemo.
My surgeon is at a different hospital and I think they kind of outsource the brutal frankness as well as the operation.

No one at my home hospital mentioned recurrence *at all* but I looked it up on reputable websites and got 70-90% for Stage 3. I’m Stage 3c so assume the upper end. My surgeon bluntly said it’s when not if, and when I asked my oncology team they agreed, but I think they only say when asked.
Never feel like an idiot and always celebrate an all clear. I will celebrate mine (if I get an NED after my forthcoming scan, which I think I will). But I will celebrate with caution.

I think that I’ll probably have a couple of years without having to worry, in that I think the chemo has been really effective (judging by numbers and the amount of *dead* cells found during surgery.)

I know that for my cancer, the PARP inhibitors are now really good. But I am poised - and of course this time I’ll spot the symptoms quickly if/when.

I’m sorry yours came back, and so quickly.

So if someone tells you their cancer has an extremely high recurrence rate, it’s actually neither helpful nor reassuring to question that narrative. It helps me to be low-key and realistic. I’m glad chemo is over but I’m keeping a level head because I know I may be back there in maybe 2-5 years. 2/
